item.title: Delivery Surge: 186 Stocks Show Strong Institutional Activity This Week
item.description: This week’s market activity was marked by significant institutional participation, as evidenced by a surge in delivery volumes and traded values across a broad range of stocks. Despite the absence of extreme delivery-only buyer or seller patterns, the high volume and value confirmations highlight a balanced but active market environment. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: Breakout Alert: 71 Bullish Technical Signals Shape This Week’s Market Moves
item.description: This week’s technical landscape was marked by a predominance of bullish patterns, signalling a positive market bias across multiple sectors and market capitalisations. The surge in moving average crossovers and gap ups suggests sustained momentum, while the absence of significant weakness patterns points to resilience among stocks near recent highs. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: Volume Explosion: 88 Stocks Lead Trading Frenzy and What It Means Next Week
item.description: This week witnessed an extraordinary surge in trading volumes across multiple sectors, with 88 stocks registering exceptional activity. The predominance of upper circuit hits over lower circuit events signals a strong bullish momentum, particularly concentrated in banking, telecom, and power sectors. Understanding these volume patterns offers valuable insights into market sentiment and potential opportunities for the coming week. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: Rating Revisions Surge: 416 Upgrades and 194 Downgrades Across Key Sectors This Week
item.description: This week witnessed a notable surge in stock score adjustments, with a total of 610 grade changes reflecting a dynamic market environment. The Non Banking Financial Company (NBFC) sector emerged as a focal point for both upgrades and downgrades, while Pharmaceuticals & Biotechnology and Garments & Apparels also featured prominently in rating revisions. Time: 17 minutes ago

item.title: 147 Stocks Hit 52-Week Highs as NBFC and Heavy Electrical Lead Rally
item.description: This weekly saw a pronounced surge in price strength across several key sectors, notably Non Banking Financial Companies (NBFC), Heavy Electrical Equipment, and Pharmaceuticals & Biotechnology. The market exhibited a clear bias towards large-cap stocks, with a substantial number of price triggers signalling upward momentum. Meanwhile, a smaller subset of stocks faced downward pressure, highlighting selective weakness within certain industries. Time: 17 minutes ago
